categories.label: Mother
categories.description: Words about mothers or mother-like roles
morph_hints: Root seen in mother-related words (wikáwəsso = she is a mother)
Suffix pattern: act/behave like… (-amto = act like)

categories.label: Father
categories.description: Words about fathers, stepfathers, or father-like roles
morph_hints: Root seen in father-related words (míhtαkʷso = he has a father)

categories.label: Daughter
categories.description: Words about daughters
morph_hints: Ending on wətosin: she is his daughter

categories.label: Son
categories.description: Words about sons, adoption, or son-in-law
morph_hints: Prefix nə- (I) + …αnk pattern in adoption/son-making verbs

categories.label: Brother
categories.description: Words about brothers; older/younger sibling order
morph_hints: Prefix kči- often marks big/elder (kči-pétαki = Big Thunderer, oldest brother)

categories.label: Sister
categories.description: Words about sisters

categories.label: Wife
categories.description: Words about wives or female spouses
morph_hints: Feminine ending seen on many female role words

categories.label: Husband
categories.description: Words about husbands or male spouses

categories.label: Spouse / partner
categories.description: Marriage, separation, or having a spouse

categories.label: Child
categories.description: Words about children (young people)
morph_hints: Diminutive/younger ending seen on child-related forms

categories.label: In-law
categories.description: Son-in-law, daughter-in-law, bride's parents

categories.label: Widow / widower
categories.description: Words for widow or widower

categories.label: Relative / kin
categories.description: Having relatives, kinship respect
english_patterns: \brespect for him as a parent or relative\b
morph_hints: Suffix pattern in relative/kinship verbs (…αkomα = I have him for a relative)

categories.label: Older than
categories.description: Being older, elder, exceeding in years
morph_hints: Compound: I am older/younger than… (compare age)

categories.label: Younger than
categories.description: Being younger than someone

categories.label: My (possession)
categories.description: First-person possession — my, from my…
morph_hints: Prefix nə- / nəta- often marks first person I (seen in nəta…, nə… forms)
Initial ni- sometimes marks my / from me

categories.label: His (possession)
categories.description: Third-person masculine possession
morph_hints: Prefix wə- appears in his/her forms (e.g. wətosin = she is his daughter)

categories.label: Her (possession)
categories.description: Third-person feminine possession
morph_hints: wə- prefix in some his/her possessed forms

categories.label: Feminine ending (-kʷe / -skʷe)
categories.description: Headwords ending in -kʷe or -skʷe — often feminine nouns or female people
morph_hints: Feminine ending — common on words for women and feminine roles
Female person marker in ethnic/tribal words (…wəskʷe = woman)

categories.label: Female person words
categories.description: Dictionary entries explicitly about women, wives, mothers, daughters, widows
morph_hints: Check for feminine ending on the Penobscot word

categories.label: Male person words
categories.description: Dictionary entries explicitly about men, fathers, brothers, husbands, widowers
